services.name: Business Consulting

services.name: Cloud Accounting & Data Storage
services.description: Your books, reports, and financial data secured in the cloud and accessible from anywhere. Ransomware and drive failure protection with everything audit-ready.

construction.para1: Construction accounting is one of the most complex and most mishandled areas of small business finance. Job costing, AIA billing, certified payroll, retainage tracking, and subcontractor management require a bookkeeper who has actually worked inside the construction industry — not one who learned it from a manual.
construction.para2: Hharpp has specialized in construction cost accounting for over a decade. Before founding Hharpp in 2009, Heather spent years inside one of the largest construction companies in Southern California. That hands-on experience is what separates our construction clients from the rest — and why their projects stay profitable.
services: Job costing by project, phase, and cost code
AIA progress billing and retainage tracking
QuickBooks and BuilderTrend setup for contractors
Cash flow management for project-based businesses

differentiators.title: 100% Virtual — Built for Remote
differentiators.body: Working entirely online since 2009 — long before virtual was an industry buzzword. Your location doesn't matter. Nevada, California, or anywhere with internet access, we manage your books, run your payroll, and review your financials with you on a video call.

differentiators.title: Relationship-First, Not Transactional
differentiators.body: We take time to understand your business, your industry, and your goals before we touch your books. Clients who have been with us for years say it feels like having a financial partner inside the business, not an outside vendor.

differentiators.title: Real Credentials, Real Expertise
differentiators.body: Heather holds an MBA, is a Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB), a retired Certified Management Accountant (CMA), and a QuickBooks Certified Advanced Pro-Advisor. She is also a member of SHRM and AIPB. In a market full of self-described bookkeepers, credentials matter.

differentiators.title: Construction Specialist
differentiators.body: Job costing, AIA billing, certified payroll, and construction-specific cash flow are not skills most bookkeepers have. Hharpp has spent over a decade building a practice around construction — it is our deepest specialty and our strongest competitive advantage.

testimonials.quote: Like Mary Poppins, she swept in and took care of everything. She set us up on QuickBooks, organized our customers and jobs, and made sense out of the chaos.

faqs.question: What does a virtual bookkeeper do for a small business?
faqs.voice_answer: Hharpp Business Services manages your financial records entirely online — handling bookkeeping, payroll, QuickBooks, and cash flow so you never have to worry about your books.
faqs.full_answer: A virtual bookkeeper manages your financial records entirely online — recording transactions, reconciling accounts, preparing financial statements, and keeping your books audit-ready — without ever stepping foot in your office. At Hharpp, we handle everything from monthly bookkeeping and catch-up work to payroll processing, QuickBooks setup, and cash flow management. You share access to your accounting software and bank feeds, and we take care of the rest. Virtual bookkeeping gives small businesses access to a full-time-quality financial professional at a fraction of the cost of an in-house hire.

faqs.question: How do I know if my small business needs a bookkeeper?
faqs.voice_answer: You need a bookkeeper if you're spending hours on your books each month, behind on reconciliations, or unsure what your business actually made last quarter.
faqs.full_answer: Your business needs a bookkeeper if you are spending more than a few hours each month on your books, falling behind on reconciliations, unsure of your actual cash position, or dreading tax season because your records aren't in order. Other clear signs include: you've received notices from the IRS or EDD, you're behind on payroll tax deposits, or you're making financial decisions without reliable numbers in front of you. The cost of a professional bookkeeper is almost always less than the penalties, missed deductions, and poor decisions that come from doing it yourself or letting it slide.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a bookkeeper, an accountant, and a CPA?
faqs.voice_answer: A bookkeeper records your transactions, an accountant analyzes them, and a CPA is a licensed professional who can represent you before the IRS. Most small businesses need a bookkeeper first.
faqs.full_answer: A bookkeeper records and organizes your financial transactions on an ongoing basis — it is the foundation of everything else. An accountant analyzes those records to produce reports, manage compliance, and offer financial strategy. A CPA (Certified Public Accountant) is a state-licensed accountant who can also represent you before the IRS, conduct audits, and sign off on tax returns. Most small businesses need a bookkeeper as their primary financial support, with a CPA engaged at tax time. Hharpp's founder, Heather Potvin, is a Certified Public Bookkeeper (CPB) and retired Certified Management Accountant (CMA), which means she brings accountant-level expertise to bookkeeping work.

faqs.question: Does Hharpp serve businesses outside of Nevada?
faqs.voice_answer: Yes — Hharpp is 100% virtual and serves small businesses across Nevada, California, and anywhere in the United States with internet access.
faqs.full_answer: Yes. Hharpp is a fully virtual bookkeeping and payroll service, which means we work with clients anywhere in the United States. While we are based in Boulder City, Nevada, and have deep experience with Nevada-specific compliance — including the Modified Business Tax (MBT), Commerce Tax, and state payroll requirements — our cloud-based model means your location is not a barrier. We currently serve clients throughout Nevada and California, with the capability to serve businesses in any state.

faqs.question: How does Hharpp work with clients virtually?
faqs.voice_answer: Hharpp connects to your QuickBooks or accounting software securely online, handles your books on a set schedule, and meets with you monthly by video call to review your numbers.
faqs.full_answer: Everything we do is managed through cloud-based accounting software — primarily QuickBooks Online, but also AccountingSuite, BuilderTrend, and Sage depending on your business. You grant us secure access to your accounting platform and bank feeds, and we handle recording, reconciliation, and reporting on your agreed schedule. We communicate via email, phone, and video conference — monthly review meetings are conducted virtually so you always understand your numbers. You can monitor your books in real time through your own software login. No in-person visits required — and no disruption to your operations.

faqs.question: What is job costing and why does it matter for construction businesses?
faqs.voice_answer: Job costing tracks every labor, material, and overhead cost against a specific project so construction businesses know exactly which jobs are profitable and which are losing money.
faqs.full_answer: Job costing is the process of tracking every expense — labor, materials, subcontractors, equipment, and overhead — against a specific project or job, so you know exactly how profitable each project is in real time. For construction businesses, job costing is critical because costs are incurred across multiple projects simultaneously, contracts span months or years, and a single cost overrun can wipe out a project's margin. Hharpp specializes in construction job costing using QuickBooks and BuilderTrend, giving Nevada contractors real-time visibility into which jobs are making money and which are not.

heather_bio: Heather Potvin founded Hharpp in 2009 at the height of the recession, turning a single vendor relationship into a practice that has helped hundreds of small businesses get organized, stay compliant, and grow with confidence. She holds an MBA, is a Certified Public Bookkeeper, a retired Certified Management Accountant, and a QuickBooks Certified Advanced Pro-Advisor. She is also a member of SHRM and AIPB. Over 30 years of accounting, management, and problem-solving experience — including deep expertise in the construction industry — means Hharpp clients get more than clean books. They get a trusted adviser who has seen almost every mistake a business can make, and knows exactly how to fix it.

lead_magnet.description: Monthly, quarterly, and annual tasks every Nevada business owner needs — with Nevada-specific compliance notes on MBT, DETR, Commerce Tax, and the 2026 sales tax deadline change. Prepared by Heather Potvin, CPB.
bullets: 40+ tasks across monthly, quarterly, annual cadences

construction.title: Construction accounting + job costing
construction.body: You need a bookkeeper who actually understands construction — not one who learned it from a manual. Hharpp has specialized in construction cost accounting for over a decade, including job costing, AIA billing, certified payroll, and BuilderTrend setup for Nevada contractors.

catchup.title: Catch-up bookkeeping + full-service support
catchup.body: Getting behind on books is more common than you think — and no situation is too far back to fix. Hharpp specializes in catch-up and clean-up work, reconstructing records from bank statements and prior data, then transitioning to clean monthly bookkeeping.

newbiz.title: New business setup + QuickBooks onboarding
newbiz.body: Starting right matters more than starting fast. Hharpp sets up your chart of accounts, configures QuickBooks for your specific industry, establishes payroll if you're hiring, and gives you the financial foundation to grow without cleaning up a mess later.

payroll.title: Payroll compliance + bookkeeping
payroll.body: Nevada payroll compliance — Modified Business Tax, DETR registration, quarterly filings — is genuinely complex for small businesses. Hharpp manages the entire payroll cycle, from employee onboarding through year-end W-2s, ensuring full state and federal compliance.

cashflow.title: Cash flow management + virtual CFO services
cashflow.body: If you're never sure whether you can make payroll next month, you need more than bookkeeping — you need a financial partner. Hharpp's virtual CFO service provides real-time cash flow visibility, forecasting, and business consulting to help you make decisions with confidence.

quickbooks.title: QuickBooks setup, cleanup + training
quickbooks.body: A misconfigured QuickBooks file causes more financial damage than no QuickBooks at all. As a QuickBooks Certified Advanced Pro-Advisor, Heather diagnoses exactly what's wrong, fixes it, and trains you or your staff to use the system correctly going forward.

fullservice.title: Full-service bookkeeping + business consulting
fullservice.body: You need a trusted financial partner who can see the whole picture. Hharpp's full-service engagement covers bookkeeping, payroll, QuickBooks, cash flow, and strategic consulting — giving you CFO-level support at a fraction of the cost of a full-time hire.

quiz.routing_logic: if q2===construction → construction | elif q1===behind OR q1===messy → catchup | elif q1===starting → newbiz | elif q3===payroll → payroll | elif q3===cashflow → cashflow | elif q3===quickbooks → quickbooks | else → fullservice
